title: Bridge Essay: Brain-breathing interaction during MRI-related anxiety.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Breath/Soul** (Phase 2) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Breath/Soul to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Brain-breathing interaction during MRI-related anxiety.** — Clin Neurophysiol. PMID: 40818329. DOI: 10.1016/j.clinph.2025.2110961. Nasal breathing can entrain fast oscillations in the prefrontal cortex and limbic systems, as experiments with rodents and intracranial EEG recordings in patients have shown. Recently, it was demonstrated that the activity of the amygdala and hippocampus can also be studied non-invasively, using functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI).
